rule: hit iff kw>=2 OR sem>=0.7 OR func>=0.7; total_hits>=1 -> FAIL

results.note: source-domain (Cherokee syllabary itself); excluded from LLM-side hit accounting
STAD metric + syllabification-tokenization alignment + IPA fine-tuning = direct twin of syllable-aware phonological tokenization
linguistically-motivated syllable-based tokenizers for low-resource languages (Swahili) = direct twin
language-specific tokenizer design = same artefact category
Ukrainian-specific tokenization efficiency = language-specific tokenizer adjacency above threshold
rare-script glyph decipherment; adjacent (different surface artefact: glyphs not syllables) but both scores meet the 0.70 threshold
Glyph visual-text compression; visual-token artefact, below both thresholds
PhonologyBench = phonological skill eval, adjacent eval primitive at threshold

fail_reason: 6 LLM-side results meet sem>=0.7 AND func>=0.7 (ranks 2,3,4,5,6,8). The candidate SYLLABARY-VOCAB regime (syllable-granularity, small fixed-size, language-specific, from-scratch tokenizer) is densely surrounded by existing prior art: STAD + IPA syllable-alignment fine-tuning (rank 2), syllable-based tokenizers for low-resource languages such as Swahili (rank 3), language-specific tokenizer design literature (rank 4), Ukrainian-specific tokenization efficiency (rank 5), rare-script glyph tokenization (rank 6), and PhonologyBench phonological evaluation (rank 8). Rank 2 alone is a near-direct twin (syllabification-tokenization alignment + phonological tokenization for LMs). Rule: total_hits>=1 -> FAIL; here total_hits=6, far exceeding threshold. Verification confirms the prior gating decision (07_hit_miss also recorded 6 hits).
